skill.name: visual-engine-skill
skill.description: Guides visual experience work for landing pages, galleries, demos, and Three.js components with accessibility and motion safety constraints.
skill.summary: Build accessible visual experiences with curated animations, demos, and Three.js components.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, a weak-crypto keyword match, and reconnaissance patterns, but these are false positives in context. The file contains guidance for visual UI work and does not include shell execution, cryptography, system probing, network access, or prompt injection. The remaining low risk is the declared Write tool, which can modify project files during intended design implementation work.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown File Path Backticks
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the marked lines are Markdown inline code for file paths and configuration names. No command execution syntax or user-controlled shell invocation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is limited to Markdown-formatted paths and a CSS media feature name. There is no Ruby code, shell command, subprocess call, or executable script in the file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptography at the description line, but the line describes a Visual Experience Engine. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, encryption key, or security protocol is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is natural language metadata about visual design behavior. I found no evidence of DES, MD5, SHA1, or related weak cryptographic usage.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported reconnaissance on safety and performance guidance lines. These lines describe animation safety and performance constraints, not host inspection or system information collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are plain English constraints about flashing effects and blocking scripts. They do not reference operating system commands, environment data, hostnames, users, or process inspection.

low_findings.title: Project File Write Capability
low_findings.description: The skill declares the Write tool, which can modify files while implementing visual UI changes. This capability is expected for the stated purpose, but users should review changes before applying them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The tool declaration explicitly includes Write, so filesystem modification capability is clear. The skill scope is UI implementation, which makes this capability legitimate but still relevant to user review.

content.value_statement: Teams need polished visual pages without unsafe motion or inconsistent animation patterns. This skill guides accessible animation systems, demos, galleries, and Three.js components.
actual_capabilities: Defines scope for animation presets, orchestrators, visual components, and demo pages.
Guides addition of named animation styles and reusable visual presets.
Requires reduced-motion support for animated experiences.
Sets safety limits for flashing, zoom, rotation, and animation duration.
Covers Three.js visual components and client-facing demo flows.
limitations: The skill only contains guidance and does not include executable helper scripts.
It assumes the target project has matching visual engine paths and pages.
It does not define exact animation code or component APIs.
It cannot verify visual quality without running or inspecting the target application.

use_cases.title: Add a Landing Page Animation
use_cases.description: Use the skill to add a named animation preset that respects reduced-motion settings and avoids unsafe movement.

use_cases.title: Curate Client Inspiration Flows
use_cases.description: Use the skill to organize visual examples that show clients how a future site could feel and behave.

use_cases.title: Review Motion Safety
use_cases.description: Use the skill to check flashing, zoom, rotation, duration, and performance constraints before release.

prompt_templates.title: Review Current Visual Scope
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the visual engine files and explain which pages, components, and animation areas this skill should affect.

prompt_templates.title: Add an Accessible Preset
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a new human-friendly animation preset for hero sections. Ensure it supports prefers-reduced-motion and avoids rapid flashing.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Demo Experience
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the demos page so visual examples are easier to scan, compare, and reuse across client presentations.

prompt_templates.title: Extend Three.js Visuals
prompt_templates.prompt: Create or refine a Three.js visual component for the Visual Experience Engine. Keep performance, motion safety, and responsive layout in scope.

output_examples.input: Add a subtle card hover effect for the inspiration gallery.
output: A named card animation preset with restrained motion.
Reduced-motion behavior that disables or simplifies movement.
A short note explaining affected gallery components.

output_examples.input: Review the visual engine page for unsafe motion.
output: A checklist covering flashing, zoom, rotation, duration, and performance.
Specific recommended changes for risky animations.
A summary of areas that already meet the skill constraints.

output_examples.input: Create a client-facing demo for background effects.
output: A demo concept using particles, gradients, or light sweeps.
Clear naming for the visual style and preset category.
Implementation notes for responsive layout and motion preferences.

best_practices: Start by locating the existing animation registry and matching its naming patterns.
Test every animated experience with reduced-motion settings enabled.
Keep visual effects purposeful, performant, and tied to the page goal.
anti_patterns: Do not add rapid flashing, intense zoom, or disorienting rotation effects.
Do not create one-off animations when a reusable preset fits the system.
Do not ignore mobile layout, performance, or accessibility constraints.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build visual experiences for landing pages, inspiration galleries, demos, and Three.js components.

faq.question: Does it include executable scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown guidance file and does not include helper scripts or compiled code.

faq.question: Why is the risk level low?
faq.answer: The static findings were false positives, but the skill declares Write access for intended project file edits.

faq.question: Can it support accessibility reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It explicitly requires reduced-motion support and limits unsafe flashing, zoom, rotation, and duration.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should users review after using it?
faq.answer: Users should review changed UI files, animation behavior, performance impact, and reduced-motion behavior.
